programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Holy Island
programme.desc: The pilgrims continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral, where they are introduced to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel meets a man in Carnoustie renovating a home for him, his dog and a menagerie of other animals to live in. Dion decides it's not safe for him to go into a house in Merseyside.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple look for a Somerset home for their young family that has room for chickens, llamas and maybe a pig. Denise Nurse lines up some wonderful rural options.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In a special programme, Monty gets an exclusive preview of a brand new garden created in Regent's Park, London, to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 6 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The Flog It! team visit Herstmonceux Castle, where antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and James Lewis find treasures to take to auction, including some beautiful bronze sculptures.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael arrives in the home of Japanese porcelain, Arita, where a railway line runs straight through the town's stunning ceramic Shinto shrine.

programme.title: Great British Menu: The Finals
programme.desc: The eight chefs compete for the last place on the banquet menu celebrating the British film industry. Chocolat author Joanne Harris is the guest judge.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Noah, Bethany and Ally pull out the stops at their local show, Lucy and Adam have a new baby and a new business plan, and Callum receives a surprise award.

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Mudeford, on the Dorset coast, a search for a missing kayaker quickly escalates when it emerges that there are six more kayaks unaccounted for.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul, Bob and Ted travel to the historic fishing spot of Driffield Beck in East Yorkshire.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh travels to Rwanda to discover its unique culture and incredible wildlife. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights from the sixth day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Great British Menu: The Finals
programme.desc: Eight regional champions cook their main courses in a bid to secure a place at a banquet celebrating the British film industry. Director Sir Stephen Frears is guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu: The Finals
programme.desc: The eight chefs compete for the last place on the banquet menu celebrating the British film industry. Chocolat author Joanne Harris is the guest judge.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: At the peak of fame, Michael is accused of abuse, and his business empire risks collapse. Contains scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dom revisits stone carver Louise and stained-glass artist Charlotte to find out how they have built thriving businesses and how much they earn after leaving his business boot camp.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie encourages distillery manager Luke in Bristol to replace coffee and energy drinks with black tea to keep stress at bay.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan fulfils some sporting dreams on the hurling pitch and catches up with his brother Gary to head out for some fishing on Killarney's beautiful Lough Leane.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches. Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quickfire quiz where six plucky contestants must stay out of the light to be alright. Can anyone beat the lightning and win the £3,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tries the deadly delicacy of pufferfish in the harbour town of Shimonoseki and visits Hitachi's Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden, with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There is a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will bear fruit later in the year.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is adding a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Banquet Special
programme.desc: In the final episode, which chef will be voted champion of champions for 2026.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman introduces Suzi Ruffell and Loyiso Gola to the stage. Contains some strong language and adult humour.

programme.title: Newsnight, followed by Weather
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley. Followed by Weather.

programme.title: Don't Look Now
programme.desc: Acclaimed mystery about a couple grieving the loss of their daughter in a tragic accident. Contains some strong language, explicit sexual content, some violence an..

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights from the seventh day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Antisemitism: Why British Jews Are Afraid - Panorama
programme.desc: Judith Moritz examines how abuse aimed at Jewish communities has become more widespread. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Great British Menu: Banquet Special
programme.desc: In the final episode, which chef will be voted champion of champions for 2026.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: BBC Eye follows Kenyans at the centre of a legal battle to track down their British soldier fathers. And Selin Girit reports on Turkey's reaction to a widening regional conflict.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Millie the Squid devises a set of tough penguin games to test the Piripenguins, Pancake and Nugget must stick together and rely on their friendship to succeed.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard race against time to retrieve sun googles so they can watch a cosmic triple eclipse.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Chipmunk pretends to be injured so that the ducklings look after him.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are made to clean the Cave of Mirrors as punishment for frolicking before a quest is complete, and they accidentally release the Party Harpy.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings: World Tour
programme.desc: Lost Object. The Lemmings use cans and tins to build a beach slide, which annoys Grizzy.
